methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $500M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Locus Robotics, founded by Bruce Welty and Al Dekin, is a Series F robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $2.0B post-money, having raised $438M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 same-sector comparables across similar valuation tiers, comprising 8 private companies and 1 public listing, with median valuations ranging from $500M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F-to-next-stage advancement to the $2.0B base, implying $2.6B. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company robotics cohort yields $1.8B, with sampled comparables including 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), and Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B).

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $1.5B to $3.0B, with a median of $2.2B. The current $2.0B post-money sits within this range but toward the lower bound, suggesting modest upside under stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in the peer set (10x spread from floor to ceiling) and heterogeneity in stage and funding histories across comparables, which limits precision of peer-median estimates. The analysis supports a range centered near $2.2B.
